text: the fog thickens — you cannot see ten paces ahead
a bell tolls in the distance
the air goes cold for no reason you can name
the smell of sulfur seeps up through the floorboards
the walls are warm to the touch — warmer than they should be
a sound beneath sound hums through the stone
the angles of the room do not agree with themselves
somewhere a door opens that should not be here
the silence has weight — it presses against your ears like water
